mechanism: Resistance Reversal (Afatinib targets EGFR T790M resistance mutation, while Palbociclib inhibits CDK4/6 to prevent MYC-driven proliferation)
supporting_evidence: Afatinib targets EGFR T790M resistance mutation: (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25730419). Palbociclib inhibits CDK4/6 to prevent MYC-driven proliferation: (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25598355). Overlapping toxicities of Afatinib and Palbociclib: (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26644493) and (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25730419).
plausibility: Moderate. Reason: Afatinib targets EGFR T790M resistance mutation, which is a common mechanism of resistance in breast cancer. Palbociclib inhibits CDK4/6 to prevent MYC-driven proliferation, which is also relevant in breast cancer. However, the combination may have overlapping toxicities such as dermatitis and diarrhoea.
combination_toxicity_risk: Moderate. Reason: Both Afatinib and Palbociclib have overlapping toxicities such as dermatitis and diarrhoea. Additionally, Afatinib has a risk of increased alanine aminotransferase levels, which may be exacerbated by Palbociclib's hepatotoxicity.
critique: The combination of Afatinib and Palbociclib shows promise in reversing resistance to EGFR inhibitors in breast cancer. However, the overlapping toxicities and potential for increased liver enzyme levels require careful monitoring and dose adjustment.
synergy: The given drug combination Afatinib + Palbociclib has highest synergy score [ZIP: 27.07, Bliss: 27.98, Loewe: 3.91, HSA: 7.41, Consensus: 16.59] in cell line DIPG25 and lowest synergy score [ZIP: 27.07, Bliss: 27.98, Loewe: 3.91, HSA: 7.41, Consensus: 16.59] in cell line DIPG25.

mechanism: Synthetic Lethality (Lapatinib targets EGFR, while Fulvestrant inhibits ER-alpha, exploiting the MYC-driven estrogen receptor-positive subtype)
supporting_evidence: Lapatinib targets EGFR, while Fulvestrant inhibits ER-alpha, exploiting the MYC-driven estrogen receptor-positive subtype (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2921114/). The combination of Lapatinib and Fulvestrant has been shown to be effective in preclinical models of breast cancer (https://www.nature.com/articles/ncomms1053). However, the safety profile of this combination requires further investigation (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2921114/).
plausibility: Moderate. The combination of Lapatinib and Fulvestrant targets different pathways in breast cancer cells, exploiting the MYC-driven estrogen receptor-positive subtype. However, both drugs have overlapping toxicities such as gastrointestinal disorders, hepatobiliary disease, and infection.
combination_toxicity_risk: Moderate. Predicted based on individual profiles. The combination of Lapatinib and Fulvestrant may increase the risk of gastrointestinal disorders, hepatobiliary disease, and infection due to their overlapping toxicities.
critique: The combination of Lapatinib and Fulvestrant is a promising approach for treating breast invasive carcinoma, but further studies are needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety. The mechanism of synthetic lethality is well-established in the literature, but the specific combination of these two drugs requires more research to confirm its benefits.
synergy: The given drug combination Lapatinib + Fulvestrant has highest synergy score [ZIP: 8.27, Bliss: 16.84, Loewe: 15.71, HSA: -0.01, Consensus: 10.20] in cell line UACC-257 and lowest synergy score [ZIP: -2.43, Bliss: -2.53, Loewe: -28.20, HSA: -7.58, Consensus: -10.18] in cell line SK-OV-3.

mechanism: Resistance Reversal (Neratinib targets EGFR T790M resistance mutation, while Buparlisib inhibits PI3K/AKT to prevent MYC-driven proliferation)
supporting_evidence: Neratinib targets the EGFR T790M resistance mutation, which is a common mechanism of resistance to EGFR inhibitors [1]. Buparlisib inhibits PI3K/AKT signaling pathway, which is involved in MYC-driven proliferation [2]. The combination of these two drugs may be effective in reversing resistance and inhibiting cancer cell growth. However, the safety profile of this combination needs to be carefully evaluated in clinical trials. (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25715113) (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26676119)
plausibility: Moderate. The combination of Neratinib and Buparlisib targets different pathways involved in cancer cell proliferation and survival, which is biologically plausible.
combination_toxicity_risk: Predicted based on individual profiles. Both drugs have overlapping toxicities such as diarrhea, nausea, and fatigue. However, the risk can be mitigated with proper dosing and patient monitoring.
critique: The combination of Neratinib and Buparlisib is a promising approach for treating breast invasive carcinoma, especially in patients with EGFR T790M resistance mutation. However, further clinical trials are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
synergy: No direct data in DrugComb database for this drug combination.

mechanism: Synthetic Lethality (Osimertinib targets EGFR T790M resistance mutation, while Everolimus inhibits mTOR, exploiting the MYC-driven mTOR-positive subtype)
supporting_evidence: Osimertinib targets EGFR T790M resistance mutation: (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25730168). Everolimus inhibits mTOR: (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19307519). MYC-driven mTOR-positive subtype in Breast Invasive Carcinoma: (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4441114/). Overlapping toxicities of Osimertinib and Everolimus: (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25730168) and (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19307519).
plausibility: Moderate. Reason: Osimertinib targets EGFR T790M resistance mutation, while Everolimus inhibits mTOR, exploiting the MYC-driven mTOR-positive subtype in Breast Invasive Carcinoma.
combination_toxicity_risk: Predicted based on individual profiles. Reason: Both drugs have overlapping toxicities such as hepatotoxicity and dermatological reactions (e.g., xeroderma, cheilitis).
critique: The combination of Osimertinib + Everolimus may be effective in targeting the MYC-driven mTOR-positive subtype in Breast Invasive Carcinoma. However, careful monitoring for overlapping toxicities such as hepatotoxicity and dermatological reactions is necessary.
synergy: No direct data in DrugComb database for this drug combination.
